title: task
description:
rust/lichen-link/src/link_layer.rs:330-338 - build_frame() accepts a dst_addr parameter but always sets addr_mode to AddrMode::None. This causes:
1. The dst_addr bytes are written to the frame (in write_to)
2. The LLSec byte indicates addr_mode=None (0 address bytes expected)
3. On parse, the receiver reads addr_mode=None and interprets dst_addr bytes as payload
4. Signature verification fails because signable data was computed with different dst_addr

Failure scenario: Any call to build_frame() with non-empty dst_addr produces an invalid frame that cannot be verified by the receiver. Currently only works for broadcast (empty dst_addr).

Fix: Either derive addr_mode from dst_addr.len() or accept addr_mode as a parameter.
close_reason: Already fixed: addr_mode is now derived from dst_addr.len() via AddrMode::from_addr_len() at lines 330-331. All 56 tests pass.
